title: Diagnose inference latency spikes
description: Localize tail latency to queueing, batch window, memory pressure, or cold paths using server and accelerator metrics.
body: Steps: 1. Confirm the spike in p99/p99.9, not the mean. 2. Decompose latency into queue wait, batch-form wait, and compute time. 3. If queue wait dominates, the system is under-provisioned or batching too slowly; if compute dominates, the batch is too large or the model too heavy. 4. Check for OOM-adjacent memory thrash and cold-start events correlated with scale-ups. 5. Fix the dominant term and re-measure. Rollback point: revert any batch/scale change that worsens the tail. Validation: the decomposed latency identifies one dominant cause and the fix lowers p99; tuning blindly without decomposition usually trades one bottleneck for another.
